questions.question: What scaled score is needed to pass the AIF-C01 exam?
questions.feedback: AIF-C01 is scored 100-1000 with a passing score of 700 (the SAA associate exam is 720; don't mix them up).

questions.question: Which two domains together make up more than half the exam?

answers.text: Fundamentals of Generative AI (24%) and Applications of Foundation Models (28%)
Responsible AI (14%) and Security, Compliance, and Governance (14%)
Fundamentals of AI and ML (20%) and Responsible AI (14%)

questions.feedback: Generative AI (24%) plus Applications of Foundation Models (28%) is 52%, so Bedrock, prompting, RAG, and fine-tuning deserve the most study.

questions.question: A business needs to transcribe recorded customer calls into text. Which service is the best fit?
questions.feedback: Amazon Transcribe is the pre-trained speech-to-text service. Comprehend does text analysis; training a custom model is unnecessary when a managed service already does the job.

questions.question: Which statements about AIF-C01 are true?

answers.text: It is a foundational exam aimed at people who use, not necessarily build, AI/ML
Scoring is compensatory: you pass on the overall score, not per domain
Unanswered questions are scored as incorrect
You must write and tune model code to pass

questions.feedback: AIF-C01 is a no-code foundational exam with compensatory scoring; leaving questions blank only costs you marks.
